objectives: Recognize common distal radius fracture patterns and mechanism.
Describe initial reduction, splinting, and neurovascular checks.
Identify red flags that push management toward fixation.

41 Orthobullets question mapping(s) for trauma-distal-radius-fractures (metadata only — no stems/answers)

metadata.description: Composite wrist mental model linking distal radius, DRUJ, and median nerve.
Operative fixation method for unstable distal radius patterns.
Recurrent displacement after closed reduction and casting.
Flexor or extensor tendon irritation from prominent hardware.

metadata.claim_text: Distal radius fracture management hinges on mechanism, alignment, median nerve exam, and appropriate immobilization choice.
Dorsal comminution often signals instability and higher fixation likelihood after reduction.
Radial height, inclination, and volar tilt language should anchor alignment discussions on wrist radiographs.
After reduction, document median nerve status, finger ROM, splint plan, and timing of post-reduction films.

metadata.action: Splint/cast with protected motion plan, repeat radiographs, and median nerve surveillance
metadata.trigger: Acceptable alignment after closed reduction in patient suitable for casting

metadata.description: Dorsal metaphyseal comminution suggesting instability after reduction.

metadata.claim_text: DRUJ instability after distal radius fixation can be missed if ulnar-sided wrist exam is skipped.
Hand-wrist shared anatomy hub links distal radius fractures to DRUJ, TFCC, scaphoid, and nerve compression neighborhoods.

metadata.action: Proceed with operative fixation pathway (volar ORIF when indicated) with documented median nerve exam
metadata.trigger: Unstable pattern with significant displacement, dorsal comminution, articular step-off, or failed closed reduction

metadata.claim_text: Accepting malalignment in young or high-demand patients without reassessing instability criteria.
The volar cortex serves as a key reduction reference when restoring length and volar tilt.

metadata.description: Open reduction and internal fixation of unstable distal radius fractures.
Complex regional pain syndrome after wrist injury.
Median nerve dysfunction after fracture swelling or operative injury.
Fracture of the distal radius and wrist articular region.
Loss of radial length on wrist radiographs affecting alignment goals.
Volar FCR interval approach to the distal radius.
